news.headline: AML Bitcoin founder convicted for wire fraud, money laundering. Faces up to 20 years for wire fraud and 10 years for money laundering. Sentencing set for July 22.
news.summary: Rowland Marcus Andrade, the founder of a cryptocurrency exchange linked to anti-money laundering, was found guilty of wire fraud and money laundering in a California court. In a March 12 trial, a jury convicted Andrade of two felony counts for defrauding investors through a scheme involving the AML Bitcoin cryptocurrency. Authorities had filed charges in June 2020, in addition to a civil case by the SEC against him. Andrade diverted over $2 million from the platform's proceeds to buy real estate and luxury cars. The SEC case involves political lobbyist Jack Abramoff, and Andrade faces up to 20 years for wire fraud and 10 years for money laundering. He is set for sentencing in July after being free on a $75,000 bond with travel restrictions.

news.headline: Frequency Electronics, Inc. reports Q3 2025 revenues of $18.9M & $49.8M for fiscal year, showing growth from 2024. Operating income also increases significantly. Net income rises to $15.4M for Q3 and $20.5M for fiscal year 2025, compared to $0.1M and $3.0M in 2024.
news.summary: In Mitchel Field, New York, on March 13, 2025, Frequency Electronics, Inc. (FEI) disclosed its financial performance. Revenues for the three and nine months ending on January 31, 2025, stood at around $18.9 million and $49.8 million, respectively. This marks an increase from the $13.7 million and $39.7 million seen during the same period in fiscal year 2024. Operating income for the same periods was $3.5 million and $8.5 million, a significant turnaround from the $0.5 million operating loss and $2.5 million operating income in fiscal year 2024. Net income for the three and nine months ending January 31, 2025, totaled $15.4 million ($1.60 per diluted share) and $20.5 million ($2.14 per diluted share), respectively. This is notably higher compared to the $0.1 million net income ($0.01 per diluted share) and $3.0 million net income ($0.32 per diluted share) reported for the same periods in January 31, 2024.

news.headline: Evoke Pharma reveals Q4 and FY 2024 financials along with business update. #EvokePharma #financialresults #businessupdate
news.summary: The recent report reveals a significant increase in global temperatures. The average temperature has risen by 1.2 degrees Celsius over the past 100 years. This rise is primarily attributed to human activities, such as burning fossil fuels and deforestation. If the current trend continues, scientists predict a further increase of 3-5 degrees Celsius by the end of the century. This escalation in temperatures is projected to have severe consequences, including more frequent and intense heatwaves, storms, and rising sea levels. Urgent action is required to curb greenhouse gas emissions and mitigate the impacts of climate change.

news.summary: In San Jose on March 13, 2025, city employees from unions IFPTE Local 21, MEF-AFSCME Local 101, and San Jose Firefighters IAFF Local 230 gathered to support public services amid the City Manager's hiring freeze. DigiFT launches Invesco’s $6.3B private credit token on Arbitrum, enhancing RWA utility for institutions and DeFi apps. #DeFi #Tokenization
news.summary: DigiFT digital asset exchange has unveiled Invesco's tokenized private credit strategy on Ethereum layer-2 network Arbitrum, expanding real-world assets' use cases for institutional investors. The iSNR token, tracking Invesco's private credit fund with $6.3 billion AUM, was launched on Feb 19, requiring a minimum investment of $10,000. DigiFT CEO Henry Zhang noted the increased utility of this integration for DeFi apps and institutional investors. The RWA tokenization trend is on the rise despite recent market downturns, with Securitize also introducing tokenized products and Franklin Templeton launching tokenized money funds. Industry data reveal RWAs onchain have grown by 17.5% to $18.1 billion over the past month, with private credit and US Treasury debt making up 91% of this total ($12.2 billion and $4.2 billion, respectively).

news.headline: Senate Banking Committee voted 18-6 to advance GENIUS stablecoin bill. Warren's amendments rejected. Bill aims to regulate stablecoins.
news.summary: The United States Senate Banking Committee voted 18-6 to advance the GENIUS Act, a bill aimed at regulating stablecoins. Senator Elizabeth Warren's proposed amendments, including limiting stablecoin issuance to banking institutions, were not included in the bill. Senator Tim Scott, chairman of the committee, praised the bill for promoting innovation and protecting consumers, with provisions such as one-to-one reserves and anti-money laundering compliance. The bill must still pass a vote in both chambers of Congress before reaching President Trump. Senator Bill Hagerty defended the bill against Warren's amendments, highlighting existing consumer protection, AML provisions, and recently added stricter reserve requirements. The bill is seen as a step towards merging stablecoins with the traditional financial system, with the Trump administration looking to leverage stablecoins to protect the US dollar's global reserve status. The industry views the bill as a positive development, with US-based firms expected to gain a competitive edge due to the stricter provisions.
